Luntian Ridge Holdings, Inc. (LRHI), a PH issuer on Bay City Exchange (BCX), reports a cyber‑operational incident dated 31 May 2026.
Per Archipelago Securities Board (ASB) guidance, potential MNPI was exposed: a draft H1 investor update with preliminary revenue guidance and Series J note pricing assumptions not yet public.
The incident arose from a compromised HR portal user session that also reached a shared banking‑reconciliation folder for payroll and tax withholding schedules.
Clustered PII — Ms. Laya Domingo (HR Business Partner, LRHI), born 12 May 1991, assigned to Kota Isla office, used personal handset LD‑ORCA‑A920 on guest Wi‑Fi SSID 'LRHI‑Visitor', session ref 7f2b‑VisMin‑1130.
Her cached payroll export included internal employee code EID‑LD‑0912, non‑government tax mapping RCO‑Maple‑441, and a payroll disbursement account ending 4421 at CoralBank (nickname only, not full number).
The same session displayed an unaudited Q2 net income variance of −6% versus prior guidance in a draft ASB filing, which remains under embargo.
Atty. Reo Villamor, Data Protection Officer, confirms no full card PANs, no national ID numbers, and no client portfolio statements were stored in the affected folders.
For context, a marketing officer aged 29 in the Visayan Gulf branch used a shared kiosk model CloudTune 8 last week; this reference is not linked to any identified person.
Another unrelated training dataset lists only birth years (e.g., 1988, 1994) and city‑tier tags, which are generic demographics and not identifying on their own.
Initial vector appears to be credential reuse from IP block IslaNet‑172.24.33.0/24 and a side‑loaded app 'QuickPDF 4' on LD‑ORCA‑A920.
Banking reconciliation sheets show only vendor code VEND‑AR‑771 and settlement batch BACH‑20260531‑02, with no customer names or full account details.
Operational impact: two hours of HR portal downtime, delayed remittance file to the Revenue Compliance Office sandbox by one day, no missed payroll in Luzonia Province.
Containment: access tokens revoked at 15:42 PH time; device quarantine issued; ASB pre‑clearance requested for a same‑day limited disclosure to BCX.
Notifications: staff are being informed via email and SMS; Ms. Domingo received monitoring for the CoralBank account nicknamed 'Laya Payroll' and was advised a device reset.
Contact: dpo@lrhi.ph and incident‑2026‑05@lrhi.ph; hotline +63 02 3999 0000 (internal only).
